batch_summary: At Central Perk's open mic night, the friends watch a talented singer named Leslie perform. While the group enjoys her music, Phoebe is defensive, especially when Ross humorously contrasts their musical styles. Leslie then dedicates a song to Phoebe, revealing they were former singing partners who had a falling out. Later, Leslie approaches the group to reconcile. Joey asks about her work writing commercial jingles, which Phoebe bitterly notes was the reason Leslie abandoned their partnership. Leslie sings one of her well-known jingles, leaving the group impressed but Phoebe tense.
cumulative_summary: During an open mic night at Central Perk, Ross, Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Phoebe gather to watch a performance by a singer-songwriter named Leslie. While the friends are impressed by Leslie's talent, Phoebe reacts with jealousy and sarcasm, particularly after Ross humorously contrasts Leslie's smooth style with Phoebe's chaotic approach. The tension escalates when Leslie dedicates a song to Phoebe, publicly apologizing for leaving their past singing partnership. Afterward, Leslie approaches the group to reconnect. When Joey asks Leslie about her career writing commercial jingles, Phoebe bitterly points out that Leslie abandoned her to pursue this commercial path. Leslie sings one of her catchy, recognizable jingles for the group, highlighting the unresolved resentment and history between the two former partners.

batch_summary: Leslie reveals she was fired from her jingle-writing job and asks Phoebe to reform their singing partnership. Phoebe bluntly rejects her twice, prompting Leslie to leave. Although Phoebe boasts to her friends that she is an unyielding "Ice Woman" to those who betray her, she immediately breaks down in tears, revealing her deep hurt. Meanwhile, Chandler desperately waits for the restroom and meets a charming woman named Ginger. Smitten by her playful banter, Chandler lets another man cut in line just to keep talking to her.
